statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will contain a string representing the maze, where \# represents a wall and \space represents an empty space. The second line of input will contain two integers representing the start and end positions.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether it is possible to solve the maze

constraints.description: Number of rows in the maze
Number of columns in the maze

hints.text: Think about how to correctly implement backtracking

editorial.title: Maze Solving Backtracking Bug Fix
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, we need to implement backtracking, keeping track of the positions we have already visited.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would be to try all possible paths, but this is inefficient and not required.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution would involve using backtracking to try all possible paths, and returning false when we hit an obstacle.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness can be proven by showing that the solution correctly implements backtracking and handles walls correctly.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run this solution, we could use a sample input, such as the example input provided in the prompt.

starterCode.java: public boolean solve(char[][] maze, int n, int m, int x, int y, int x2, int y2, boolean[][] visited);

    def solve(self, maze, n, m, x, y, x2, y2, visited):

functionSignature.java: public boolean solve(char[][] maze, int n, int m, int x, int y, int x2, int y2, boolean[][] visited);

functionSignature.javascript: var solve = function(maze: char[][], n: number, m: number, x: number, y: number, x2: number, y2: number, visited: Set):

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def solve(self, maze:
        n, m, x, y, x2, y2, visited):
        for dx, dy in [(1, 0), (-1, 0), (0, 1), (0, -1)]:
            nx, ny = x + dx, y + dy
            if 0 <= nx < n and 0 <= ny < m and maze[nx][ny] == \#:
                return False
        return True

if __name__== ' __main__':
    maze_input = input()
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    x, y = map(int, input().split())
    x2, y2 = map(int, input().split())
    maze = [list(map(char, input().strip())) for input_ in maze_input.split(\n)]
    visited = set()
    sol = Solution()
    result = sol.solve(maze, n, m, x, y, x2, y2, visited)
    print(result)
